title: Working Tree, Index, Commits, and Atomic History · Independent Repository Trial
summary: Turn a mixed set of feature, formatting, and accidental changes into reviewable commits without losing work. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of an atomic commit construction workbook with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace HEAD, index, and working-tree versions of a path before using status, diff, add, restore, or commit.
Read branch, staged, unstaged, untracked, rename, conflict, and ahead/behind status without treating short output as context-free.
Select working-tree, staged, commit-to-commit, path-limited, word, and summary diffs from the review question.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
lab case — README.md has one intended line, data/incidents.csv has an accidental edit, and evidence.txt is untracked. Construct one atomic commit by comparing working tree, index, and HEAD, then verify the staged diff.

What must be established before implementation?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for trace head, index, and working-tree versions of a path before using status, diff, add, restore, or commit.

content.text: README.md has one intended line, data/incidents.csv has an accidental edit, and evidence.txt is untracked. Construct one atomic commit by comparing working tree, index, and HEAD, then verify the staged diff.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Three Tree Model condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: gitworkflows Manual — Controls integration, topic branch, merge, release, and distributed maintainer workflow guidance.

hints: Name the Three Tree Model precondition or invariant that the lab case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: README.md has one intended line, data/incidents.csv has an accidental edit, and evidence.txt is untracked. Construct one atomic commit by comparing working tree, index, and HEAD, then verify the staged diff.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: git add sends files directly from the working tree into permanent history.

options.text: git add sends files directly from the working tree into permanent history. This does not resolve commit to the mission model.

For commit to the mission model, apply Three Tree Model without its stated preconditions, omit the an atomic commit construction workbook trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Three Tree Model for an atomic commit construction workbook.
State the Three Tree Model preconditions, preserve earlier contracts, and name the trace or changed-input result that will decide success.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model
steps.instruction: Write the lab evidence note for Atomic Commit Message: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — README.md has one intended line, data/incidents.csv has an accidental edit, and evidence.txt is untracked. Construct one atomic commit by comparing working tree, index, and HEAD, then verify the staged diff. steps.why: Retrieving Atomic Commit Message through the concrete an atomic commit construction workbook case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Create a cohesive commit with an imperative summary, rationale, test evidence, and no accidental generated or secret data.
